name: Prime Visa

verdict.text: Only earns its keep alongside an active Prime membership — otherwise the signature rate quietly halves

earn_rates.category: Amazon.com, Audible.com & Whole Foods Market — with Prime membership

earn_note: The headline 5% at Amazon and Whole Foods requires an active Prime membership ($139/yr or $14.99/mo, billed separately) — without it, that rate drops to 3%. This is the single most important thing to know about the card: let Prime lapse and you lose a third of its value overnight, on top of losing Prime itself. No caps on any category.

redemption_options.method: Amazon.com checkout, statement credit, bank deposit, or gift cards via Chase.com

points.note: Technically Chase Ultimate Rewards points under the hood, but on this card alone they're worth a flat 1¢ each with no travel-portal bonus. They can't be moved into a separate premium Ultimate Rewards account (Sapphire Preferred/Reserve) to unlock transfer partners — for all practical purposes, treat this as straight cash back.

transfer_partners.highlight: No transfer partners on this card. Points earned here are a separate pool from a premium Ultimate Rewards account and can't be combined with a Sapphire card's balance to reach airline/hotel partners.

insurance.detail: +1 year on eligible warranties of 3 years or less (up to 4 yrs total)

protection_note: A stronger-than-expected protection suite for a no-fee retail card, courtesy of its Visa Signature status — purchase protection, extended warranty, baggage delay, lost luggage, and travel accident insurance are all included. There's no trip cancellation/interruption coverage and no cell phone protection, so it's still not a substitute for a dedicated travel card.
rental_note: Secondary auto rental collision damage waiver (standard Visa Signature benefit) — decline the counter's CDW and pay with this card. Excludes exotic/luxury makes and rentals over 31 days; pays out after your personal auto policy.

services.name: Prime membership required for full value
services.detail: The 5% Amazon/Whole Foods rate needs an active Prime membership. Prime itself is a separate paid subscription ($139/yr or $14.99/mo) — this card doesn't include or discount it.

services.name: No foreign transaction fees
services.detail: 0% on purchases made outside the US — unusual for a no-fee retail card.

services.name: Next-day Amazon redemption
services.detail: Cash back can be applied directly at Amazon.com checkout as soon as the next day, with no minimum balance required.

additional_cards.title: Additional cards

benefits.text: Earns cash back on their spend (into your account)
Same purchase protection as the primary card

additional_cards.note: Additional cards are free — no reason not to add a trusted household member, especially one who shops Amazon or Whole Foods regularly.

timeline.text: Chase & Amazon renamed the Amazon Prime Rewards Visa Signature Card to Prime Visa (and the no-fee Amazon Rewards Visa Signature Card to Amazon Visa), alongside a design refresh and expanded 2% categories (gas, restaurants, local transit).
